channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Americans' pride in US history and democracy dropped steeply over past decade, poll finds
item.description: Americans’ pride in their country's history or the way its democracy works has dropped noticeably over the past decade, according to a new poll from the Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: DR Congo conflict: Rwandan FM 'disappointed by increasingly biased US mediation'
item.description: In an interview with FRANCE 24, Rwandan Foreign Minister Olivier Nduhungirehe said his country is "disappointed by the increasingly biased US mediation" in the conflict with DR Congo. He also accused the US of sanctioning Kigali alone while Kinshasa fails to honour the 2025 Washington Accords between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Marc PERELMAN

item.title: Thousands of Vespas swarm Rome to mark scooter’s 80th anniversary
item.description: The Vespa was celebrating its 80th birthday on Saturday with thousands of riders parading through the historic centre of Rome to celebrate the legendary scooter, which has become an icon of the Italian way of life.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: London artist Georgia Semple explores tension between perception and reality
item.description: Her paintings are rich, layered works that feature distorted or warped figures – something that Georgia Semple says represents the "loss of perception" that one experiences throughout one's life. Speaking to FRANCE 24, the London-based artist explains how themes of faith and addiction as well as her Guyanese heritage are expressed in her thought-provoking works. She says she hopes her paintings might prompt viewers to question "their own truths" or "how faith might show up in their lives".
item.dc:creator: Carys GARLAND

item.title: 'Pre-existing vulnerabilities: Venezuela already experiencing economic turmoil, displacement crisis'
item.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Elinor Raikes. Vice President and Head of Program Delivery at the International Rescue Committee. According to Raikes, earthquakes expose and amplify the vulnerabilities that already exist beneath the surface of a society. She suggests that the devastation unfolding in Venezuela is not solely the consequence of seismic forces, but the collision of a major natural disaster exacerbated by economic collapse, displacement, weakened public services, and shrinking humanitarian funding. Rakes warns the earthquake is not a short-term emergency but rather the beginning of a prolonged humanitarian crisis. She emphasises the logistical obstacles confronting aid agencies, the importance of coordination between international and local organisations, and the reality that recovery will be measured in months rather than weeks.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Canada reach new heights as the Netherlands prepare to face Morocco
item.description: Co-hosts Canada beat South Africa 1-0 to advance in the knockout round for the first-ever time at a World Cup. Meanwhile Monday's action will see the Netherlands take on Morocco in one of the most hotly anticipated games of the Round of 32. Brazil will face Japan and Germany play Paraguay, the winner of which could play France next.
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: Ukraine strikes another Russian oil refinery as Putin admits fuel shortages
item.description: Ukraine kept up its heavy drone assault on Russia, setting fire to a major oil refinery in the south, as President Vladimir Putin acknowledged for the first time on Sunday that the country was facing a “certain deficit” of fuel and vowed to strengthen protection of oil facilities and boost fuel output.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Rescue window closing in Venezuela as quake death toll mounts
item.description: Emergency teams with rescue dogs were searching Sunday for any remaining survivors of powerful twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela, where the death toll has surpassed 1,450 and the critical 72-hour window for rescuing trapped victims following a natural disaster has now passed.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: At least five killed in shooting in Stade in northern Germany, police say
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: At least five killed in shooting in Stade in northern Germany, police say
item.description:
At least five people have been killed in a shooting in the northern German city of Stade, police said Monday. Two people have been detained, including the suspected shooter.
⁠
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Thousands of foreign nationals leave South Africa ahead of June 30 'deadline'
item.description: Thousands of people have fled South Africa in recent weeks ahead of a June 30 "deadline" for foreign nationals to leave the country announced by anti-immigrant groups. We spoke to Congolese refugees in KwaZulu-Natal province who were forced to leave their homes and livelihoods after anti-immigrant protests erupted in violence.
item.dc:creator: Eunice MASSON

item.title: France's 2027 presidential election: Can the far-right National Rally win without Le Pen?
item.description: With less than a year to go until France's April 2027 presidential election, the far-right National Rally party still hasn't designated a candidate. As Marine Le Pen appeals a graft conviction that barred her from seeking office for five years, her young protégé Jordan Bardella is poised to step in. But some doubt whether the young Bardella has what it takes to win the Élysée Palace.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Nadler

item.title: World Cup 2026: Canada make history by reaching Round of 16
item.description: Canada secured a historic place in the FIFA World Cup Round of 16 with a 1-0 victory over South Africa in Los Angeles. Captain Stephen Eustáquio scored the only goal of the match deep into stoppage time. The Canucks will face the winners of Monday's tie between the Netherlands and Morocco on July 4 in Houston.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: China, EU commerce ministers meet amid widening trade imbalance
item.description: EU Trade Commissioner Maros Sefcovic and his Chinese counterpart are meeting in Brussels as Europe prepares countermeasures to battle a flood of cheap imports. Also, oil prices climb after a weekend of renewed strikes between the US and Iran, and South Korea unveils a massive new investment in chip production.
item.dc:creator: Bryan QUINN

item.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Father and son rescued as international teams help search
item.description: A ​father and his son were pulled out alive from the rubble of a ​collapsed building on Sunday, four days after the devastating earthquakes that struck Venezuela. It was a scene that gavehope to the French and U.S. rescue workers active in the ​area as ‌they race against the clock to find more survivors.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Anger and grief as hopes fade of finding Venezuela earthquake survivors
item.description: Hopes were fading Monday of finding survivors more than four days after powerful twin earthquakes struck Venezuela, as residents grow increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the disaster that has killed at least 1,450 people.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pakistan strikes eastern Afghanistan as Taliban says civilians killed
item.description: Pakistan's security forces killed at least 29 militants in ground ​and air operations along the Afghanistan border, it said on Monday, while the Afghan Taliban said at least 38 civilians were killed in airstrikes. France 24's correspodent in Islamabad, Shazaib Wahlah, as the latest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Venezuela: Mother and baby among earthquake survivors as rescue time runs out
item.description: Over 1,400 people have died following twin #earthquakes in #Venezuela on June 24. #Rescue teams are still looking for survivors, although hope is fading. The 72-hour window after natural #disasters is the key moment to find the living, according to experts.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Brutal' heatwave breaks Germany temperature record
item.description: France 24's Nick Holdsworth reports from Berline, Germany, after the country set a new temperature record on Sunday at 41.7C, according to preliminary data from the German weather service.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Canada celebrates historic win as last-gasp strike sends team into last 16
item.description: Stephen Eustaquio hammered home a rasping strike from the edge of the ​penalty area in the 92nd minute to give Canada a 1-0 victory over South Africa on Sunday, propelling the co-hosts into the World Cup last 16 for the first time in their history.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Craze for XXL snacks raises obesity fears in China
item.description: In China, a massive surge in junk food and snacks has sparked a new trend: giant retail stores dedicated entirely to XXL-sized snacks. But the craze is raising serious questions about obesity, in a country where 19 percent of children are now overweight.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Sultan of Oman to meet France's Macron with spotlight on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is set to host the Sultan of Oman at the Elysée Palace this Monday as he kicks off a two-day visit to France. The two leaders are expected to discuss regional security amid escalations in Iran and particularly securing the toll-free reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. France 24’s Matthew-Mary Caruchet takes a look at how Oman became a major diplomatic player in the region.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Israel says detonates Hezbollah tunnel, strikes south Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli army destroyed an extensive tunnel built by Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defence Minister Israel Katz said Sunday, with Lebanese state media reporting strikes in the area. The attacks came despite a trilateral framework agreement signed by Lebanon and Israel under US sponsorship on Friday to pave the way for peace between the two countries and disarm Iran-backed Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US says Iran talks to resume after both sides agree to halt strikes
item.description: Iran and the United States agreed to halt recent hostilities in the Gulf and renew talks regarding their dispute over the Strait of Hormuz, a U.S. official said on Sunday, raising hopes of saving an interim peace deal that was under pressure from days of tit-for-tat strikes. Axios, which first reported the cessation of hostilities, citing a senior U.S. official, said talks would resume Tuesday in Qatar. David Smith, The Guardian's bureau chief in Washington, has the latest.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
